title: 10 Herbal Essential Oils For Eczema
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils can be a game-changer for people dealing with eczema. Eczema is a skin condition that causes redness, itching, and dryness. Herbal teas made from certain plants can help relieve these symptoms. One of the main reasons herbal teas work is because they help reduce inflammation in the skin.  For example, Aloe barbadensis, also known as aloe vera, is a popular remedy for eczema. Its gel contains anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated skin. Drinking aloe vera tea can help reduce swelling and redness associated with eczema.  Another example is Calendula officinalis, also known as pot marigold. Its tea has antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that promote healing and reduce itching. Calendula tea can help repair the skin barrier and reduce the risk of infection.  Nettle root, or Urtica dioica, is another herb that can help with eczema. Its tea has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that reduce inflammation and promote skin health. Drinking nettle root tea can help reduce itching and redness, and promote the healing of eczema lesions.  Using herbal teas to relieve eczema can bring many benefits to your life. For one, it can reduce the need for harsh medications and treatments. It can also help you manage symptoms naturally, without relying on steroids or other medications. Additionally, herbal teas can promote overall skin health, reducing the risk of eczema flare-ups and other skin issues.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis essential oils contains bioactive constituents such as aloin, aloe-emodin, and acemannan. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce redness and swelling associated with eczema. Aloe-emodin, in particular, has been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals that exacerbate eczema symptoms. Acemannan, a polysaccharide, has soothing and moisturizing effects that help calm irritated skin and promote wound healing. By addressing inflammation and promoting skin health, aloe barbadensis essential oils can provide relief from eczema symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal essential oils for eczema
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of Aloe barbadensis leaves and 1 cup of carrier oil (coconut or jojoba oil).
Wash the Aloe leaves with 1 cup of water and dry them with a clean cloth.
Crush the Aloe leaves to release their gel and mix with 1/2 cup of carrier oil in a bowl.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a separate container. Discard the solids.
Bottle the Aloe essential oil and store it in a cool, dark place. Use 1-2 teaspoons as needed for eczema relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis essential oils contains triterpenoid saponins, flavonoids, and carotenoids. These bioactive constituents have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe and calm the skin. The triterpenoid saponins, specifically, inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, reducing redness and swelling associated with eczema. The flavonoids and carotenoids have antioxidant properties that protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, promoting healing and regeneration. By reducing inflammation and protecting the skin, Calendula officinalis essential oils can help alleviate symptoms of eczema.
remedies.image_alt: Calendula officinalis herbal essential oils for eczema
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Calendula officinalis flowers and 1 cup of carrier oil (coconut or olive oil).
Combine flowers and carrier oil in a clean glass jar. Steep for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the flowers.
Transfer the liquid to a dark glass bottle and store it in a cool, dark place. Let it sit for 2-3 weeks to mature.
After 2-3 weeks, filter the essential oil again through a cheesecloth or coffee filter. Your Calendula officinalis essential oil is now ready to use for eczema relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica essential oils contains bioactive constituents like histamine, flavonoids, and polysaccharides. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and swelling associated with eczema. Histamine, in particular, has been shown to inhibit the release of pro-inflammatory chemicals that exacerbate eczema symptoms. The flavonoids in Urtica dioica essential oils, such as quercetin and kaempferol, have antioxidant properties that help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals. By reducing inflammation and protecting the skin, Urtica dioica essential oils may help alleviate eczema symptoms and promote skin health.
remedies.image_alt: Urtica dioica herbal essential oils for eczema
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Urtica dioica leaves, 1 cup of carrier oil (coconut or olive oil), and a clean glass jar.
Wash the Urtica dioica leaves with cold water and pat them dry with a paper towel.
Combine the dried leaves and carrier oil in the glass jar, making sure the leaves are fully covered.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day. Then, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a new glass jar.
Discard the solids and store the Urtica dioica essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia essential oils contains a high concentration of bioactive constituents, including cineole, linalool, and terpinen-4-ol. These compounds have potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help reduce redness and swelling associated with eczema. Cineole and linalool also have antifungal properties, making them effective against fungal infections that can trigger eczema symptoms. Terpinen-4-ol, on the other hand, has a soothing effect on the skin, reducing itching and discomfort. By reducing inflammation and fighting off underlying infections, Melaleuca alternifolia essential oils can help alleviate eczema symptoms and promote healthy skin.
remedies.image_alt: Melaleuca alternifolia herbal essential oils for eczema
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of Melaleuca alternifolia leaves and 1 cup of carrier oil (coconut or jojoba oil).
Wash the leaves with 1 cup of water and let them dry completely.
Fill a 2-liter jar with the dried leaves and pour 2 cups of carrier oil over them.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
Strain the oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter and discard the solids. Store the oil in a dark glass bottle.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia essential oils contains linalool and linalyl acetate as its main bioactive constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to soothe and calm irritated skin. The antifungal properties of linalool and linalyl acetate also prevent the growth of bacteria that can exacerbate eczema. By reducing inflammation and preventing infection, Lavandula angustifolia essential oils can help to promote skin healing and reduce eczema symptoms. The anti-inflammatory properties of these compounds also help to reduce redness and itching associated with eczema.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal essential oils for eczema
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Lavandula angustifolia flowers and 2 cups of water in a large pot.
Heat the water over low heat for 30 minutes, then remove from heat.
Add the Lavandula angustifolia flowers to the hot water and let it steep for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a separate container. Discard the solids.
Use a dropper to transfer the oil to a glass bottle. Store the essential oil in a cool, dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea essential oils contains active constituents such as alkamides, glycosides, and phenolic acids. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce redness and swelling associated with eczema. The phenolic acids in Echinacea purpurea essential oils have antioxidant properties, which can protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals and promote healing. The alkamides and glycosides in this oil have immunomodulatory effects, which can help regulate the body's immune response and reduce inflammation. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Echinacea purpurea essential oils may be beneficial for individuals with eczema.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal essential oils for eczema
remedy_procedure: Gather materials: 2 cups of Echinacea purpurea flowers, a carrier oil (coconut or olive oil), a glass jar, a cheesecloth, and a dark glass bottle.
Dry the Echinacea purpurea flowers for 1-2 weeks to reduce moisture. Store them in a cool, dry place.
Combine 1 cup of dried Echinacea purpurea flowers with 2 cups of carrier oil in the glass jar. Stir well to mix.
Steep the mixture for 2-3 weeks in a cool, dark place, shaking the jar every day. Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a bowl.
Transfer the essential oil to a dark glass bottle and store it in a cool, dark place. Use 2-3 drops of the essential oil per application for eczema relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Chamomilla recutita essential oils contains apigenin and luteolin, which are potent anti-inflammatory compounds. These properties help reduce redness and swelling associated with eczema. The essential oil's anti-oxidant properties, contributed by constituents like bisabolol, protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals. Chamomilla recutita essential oils also possess soothing and calming properties, which can help ease the discomfort and itchiness often experienced with eczema. By reducing inflammation and promoting skin health, Chamomilla recutita essential oils can provide relief from eczema symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Chamomilla recutita herbal essential oils for eczema
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Chamomilla recutita flowers, fresh or dried.
Combine 1 cup of flowers with 2 cups of carrier oil (coconut or olive oil) in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids.
Bottle the essential oil and store it in a cool, dark place. Use 5-7 drops per application for eczema relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Saponaria officinalis essential oils contains a variety of bioactive constituents, including saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe and calm irritated skin associated with eczema. The saponins in Saponaria officinalis essential oils have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, reducing redness and swelling in eczema-affected skin. The flavonoids and phenolic acids also have potent antioxidant properties, which can help to protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals and promote healing. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Saponaria officinalis essential oils may be a useful natural remedy for managing eczema symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Saponaria officinalis herbal essential oils for eczema
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Saponaria officinalis flowers and leaves, and 1 cup of carrier oil (jojoba or sweet almond oil).
Combine the Saponaria officinalis flowers and leaves with 1 cup of carrier oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another glass jar. Discard the solids.
Store the Saponaria officinalis essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid in a cool, dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum essential oils contains flavonoids, tannins, and phenolic acids, which are the active constituents that contribute to its therapeutic properties. The flavonoids, specifically quercetin and hyperoside, have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce redness and swelling associated with eczema. The tannins in Hypericum perforatum essential oils have astringent and antiseptic properties, which help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing the risk of infection and promoting healing. The phenolic acids, such as ferulic acid and sinapic acid, have antioxidant properties that help to protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate eczema symptoms. By using Hypericum perforatum essential oils, individuals with eczema may experience reduced inflammation, improved skin health, and a decrease in the severity of their symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Hypericum perforatum herbal essential oils for eczema
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Hypericum perforatum flowers, 1 cup of carrier oil (like coconut or jojoba oil), and a clean glass jar.
Place the flowers in the jar and pour the carrier oil over them. Stir gently to make sure the flowers are fully covered.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every few days. This is called 'infusion'.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids.
Transfer the liquid to a dark glass bottle and label it. Store it in a cool, dark place. Use a few drops of the essential oil in a carrier oil or lotion to help with eczema symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Symphytum officinale essential oils contains bioactive constituents like allantoin, vitamin E, and mucilage, which contribute to its skin-soothing properties. Allantoin helps to break down dead skin cells and promote cell turnover, reducing inflammation and irritation associated with eczema. Vitamin E has antioxidant properties that protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate eczema symptoms. Mucilage, a thick, protective substance, helps to lock in moisture and soothe dry, itchy skin. These properties make Symphytum officinale essential oils a useful addition to topical treatments for eczema.
remedies.image_alt: Symphytum officinale herbal essential oils for eczema
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Symphytum officinale leaves and flowers. Clean them with water.
Place the cleaned leaves and flowers in a glass container. Add 2 cups of carrier oil, such as coconut oil or olive oil.
Steep the mixture for 2-3 weeks in a cool, dark place. Shake the container every day.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container. Discard the solids.
Bottle the essential oil and store it in a cool, dark place. Use 2-3 drops of the oil per application for eczema relief.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent eczema from forming. Some herbal teas, like peppermint and chamomile, have anti-inflammatory properties that could soothe the skin. Green tea, rich in antioxidants, may also help protect the skin from damage. These teas might reduce the risk of eczema, but more research is needed to confirm their effectiveness.
faq_safe: Consuming herbal teas for eczema daily can be safe if you choose teas that are known to help with skin issues. Some popular options include peppermint, chamomile, and calendula. These teas are usually gentle and won't cause any harm. However, it's essential to pay attention to how your body reacts and adjust your consumption accordingly.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can help with eczema symptoms, but it's hard to predict exactly when you'll see results. Some people notice a difference within a few days to a week, while others may take a few weeks or even months to see improvement. It really depends on your individual skin and the specific herbs used.
faq_time: For eczema, it's often best to drink herbal tea in the evening. This can help calm your skin and relax your body before bed. Try sipping a soothing cup of tea, like peppermint or chamomile, as you wind down for the night. This can help reduce inflammation and promote a restful night's sleep.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for eczema, but it's not always clear how. Some herbal teas, like chamomile and calendula, have properties that might affect how well eczema medications work. Others might increase the risk of side effects. If you're taking eczema medication, it's a good idea to be aware of what you're drinking and how it might affect your treatment.
faq_pregnant: When pregnant and dealing with eczema, it's essential to be cautious with herbal teas. Some herbs, like peppermint and chamomile, can be safe in moderation, but others like sage and hyssop might not be suitable. If you're considering herbal teas, research the specific herb and its potential effects on your baby.
faq_breastfeeding: Herbal teas for eczema can be a helpful addition to your skincare routine, but when breastfeeding, it's essential to be cautious. Some herbal teas, like peppermint and chamomile, are generally considered safe in moderation. However, teas like pennyroyal and sage may affect your milk supply or cause issues in your baby. Always check the ingredients and consume them in limited amounts.
title_2: Essential Oils for Eczema in Pregnant Women: 10 Natural Remedies
